query: NHTSA investigation Tesla Model 3 fatal crash Texas autonomous vehicle rollout impact
a_issues: Includes low-reliability/noisy results (YouTube, Facebook, Reddit). The strongest result says the software is what Tesla is rolling out on robotaxis this year, but does not clearly distinguish the disputed driver claim from confirmed regulator findings. Overall usable, but some snippets are less precise and add noise.
b_issues: One snippet says investigators say the Tesla was in self-driving mode, which overstates what is confirmed; the anchor indicates ADAS/FSD was engaged per recovered data, but many contemporaneous reports were based on the driver's claim. It also mentions the company released reports on some crashes, which is peripheral and slightly vague.
reasoning: Both sets contain the core facts needed: NHTSA opened a special crash investigation into a fatal Tesla Model 3 crash in Katy, Texas, involving Tesla’s automated/driver-assistance system, and both connect the probe to Tesla’s broader autonomous/robotaxi rollout. However, B does this more cleanly and directly with three mainstream news results (CNBC, NBC, Forbes). CNBC explicitly states the rollout impact: the probe comes as Musk is trying to sustain investor belief in Tesla’s autonomous-vehicle ambitions. A has the same point in NBC’s snippet about robotaxis and using the same software, but it is diluted by irrelevant or unreliable social/video/forum results. So B better enables an LLM to answer correctly from the provided content alone.
